zh.lifeSummary: Heer Pieter van Hénin Heer van Boussu是Jan van Hénin-Boussu和Catharina van Bethune的兒子，於1433年出生。他和Isabeau de Lalaing至少有4個兒子和3個女兒。他於1481年被列為ridder in de orde van het gulden vlies。他去世於1490年在法國享年57歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Heer Pieter van Hénin Heer van Boussu是Jan van Hénin-Boussu和Catharina van Bethune的儿子，于1433年出生。他和Isabeau de Lalaing至少有4个儿子和3个女儿。他于1481年被列为ridder in de orde van het gulden vlies。他去世于1490年在法国享年57岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Hénin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: variant of   Hennion  .

variants.name: Hennion
meanings: Welsh: from a hypercorrected form of the Welsh personal name Ennion, see Eynon .
Flemish and French (mainly Nord): from a pet form of the personal name Henri or Jehen (see Jean ).

nameMeanings.name: Heer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernhard, Erwin, Hans, Alfons, Alois, Ewald, Kurt, Monika, Otto.
German and Dutch: from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name with the first element hari, heri ‘army’, as, for instance, Hermann . This surname is rare in the Netherlands.
Dutch: variant, without the definite article de ‘the’, of De Heer .

variants.name: Heerema
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Egbert.
West Frisian and Dutch (Groningen): patronymic from the personal name Heer , composed with the Frisian suffix -ma ‘(one of the) men of’.

variants.name: Heeringa
meanings: West Frisian: habitational name from any of several mansions or farms so called, for instance near Dongjum and Marssum, once inhabited by a certain Heere or Hero and his kin (see Heer 1). It is formed with the patronymic suffix -ing and genitive plural ending -a.

variants.name: Hehr
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Elfriede, Erhart, Horst, Milbert.
German: variant of Heer 1 and 3, but in some instances a nickname either from hëher ‘jay’, or from Middle High German hēr ‘distinguished, proud’.

variants.name: Heeren
meanings: Dutch, North German, and East Frisian: patronymic from   Heer   1.

variants.name: Hermann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Jurgen, Mathias, Gottlieb, Horst, Juergen, Klaus, Lorenz, Bernd, Dieter, Erwin.

German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements heri, hari ‘army’ + man ‘man’. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, and some other European countries; see also below. Compare Harman 5.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): adoption of German name (see 1 above). Compare   Herman  .

variants.name: De Heer
meanings: Dutch: from heer ‘(feudal) lord, master’, with the definite article de ‘the’, probably applied as a nickname to someone who behaved in a lordly manner, or as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a feudal lord or the like. Compare Heer .

nameMeanings.name: Pieter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: Heer Pieter van Hénin Heer van Boussu was born in 1433 as the son of Jan van Hénin-Boussu and Catharina van Bethune. He had at least 4 sons and 3 daughters with Isabeau de Lalaing. In 1481, at the age of 48, his occupation is listed as ridder in de orde van het gulden vlies. He died in 1490, in France, at the age of 57.

fr.lifeSummary: Heer Pieter van Hénin Heer van Boussu est né en 1433, fils de Jan van Hénin-Boussu et Catharina van Bethune. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles avec Isabeau de Lalaing. En 1481, à l’âge de 48 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est ridder in de orde van het gulden vlies. Il est décédé en 1490, à France, à l’âge de 57 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Heer Pieter van Hénin Heer van Boussu wurde 1433, als Sohn von Jan van Hénin-Boussu und Catharina van Bethune geboren. Er hatte mit Isabeau de Lalaing mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. 1481, als er 48 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als ridder in de orde van het gulden vlies aufgeführt. Er starb 1490 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Heer Pieter van Hénin Heer van Boussu è nato il nel 1433 come figlio di Jan van Hénin-Boussu e Catharina van Bethune. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie con Isabeau de Lalaing. Nel 1481, all'età di 48 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come ridder in de orde van het gulden vlies. Morì nel 1490, nel Francia, all'età di 57 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Heer Pieter van Hénin Heer van Boussuは1433年にJan van Hénin-BoussuとCatharina van Bethuneの息子として生まれた。彼はIsabeau de Lalaingとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。1481年，48歳の時，職業はridder in de orde van het gulden vliesと記録されている。彼は1490年にフランスで57歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Heer Pieter van Hénin Heer van Boussu(은)는 1433년 Jan van Hénin-Boussu(와)과 Catharina van Bethune의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Isabeau de Lalaing(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1481년, 48세의 나이로 그의 직업은 ridder in de orde van het gulden vlies이었다. 그는 1490년 프랑스에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Heer Pieter van Hénin Heer van Boussu nasceu em 1433 filho de Jan van Hénin-Boussu e Catharina van Bethune. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas com Isabeau de Lalaing. Em 1481, com 48 anos, sua profissão aparece como ridder in de orde van het gulden vlies. Ele faleceu em 1490, em França, com 57 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Heer Pieter van Hénin Heer van Boussu родился в 1433 г. Его родителей звали Jan van Hénin-Boussu и Catharina van Bethune. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери от Isabeau de Lalaing. В 1481 году, в возрасте 48 лет,  он работал по профессии ridder in de orde van het gulden vlies. Он умер в 1490 г. в местности Франция в возрасте 57 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Heer Pieter van Hénin Heer van Boussu nació en 1433 como hijo de Jan van Hénin-Boussu y Catharina van Bethune. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas con Isabeau de Lalaing. En 1481, a la edad de 48 años, su ocupación registrada era ridder in de orde van het gulden vlies. Murió en 1490, en Francia, a la edad de 57 años.
